agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A logistics platform introducing automated route optimization for drivers at shift start, with dynamic recalculation triggers, customer notification windows, vehicle-class enforcement, and configurable stop duration. This spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, fleet/driver management, delivery lifecycle, and customer notifications.",
    "bounded_context": "Multiple bounded contexts identified: (1) Route Planning — owns route calculation, optimization, recalculation triggers, stop ordering, and ETA computation; (2) Fleet Management — owns driver profiles, shift lifecycle, vehicle classes, zone assignments, and licensing constraints; (3) Delivery Management — owns delivery lifecycle (pending, assigned, in-transit, delivered, failed), zone classification, and package characteristics including size/weight class; (4) Customer Notification — owns notification triggers, delivery proximity windows, and customer communication preferences. The 'Smart Route Optimization' feature is an orchestration across all four contexts, not a single context.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Shift", "definition": "A bounded work period for a driver, beginning when the driver clocks in and ending when they clock out or the shift window expires. Route assignment is triggered at shift start." },
      { "term": "Zone", "definition": "A geographic region used to partition deliveries and assign drivers. A driver is assigned to one zone per shift." },
      { "term": "Optimized Route", "definition": "An ordered sequence of delivery stops computed by the system to minimize total travel time/distance within a driver's zone, respecting all constraints (vehicle class, delivery windows)." },
      { "term": "Recalculation Trigger", "definition": "An event that invalidates the current optimized route and forces recomputation. Three defined triggers: new delivery added to zone, delivery marked as failed, driver deviation from suggested route." },
      { "term": "Licensed Vehicle Class", "definition": "A classification of a driver's vehicle (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight) that determines which deliveries they are eligible to carry. This is a hard constraint, not a preference." },
      { "term": "Next-3-Stops Window", "definition": "A proximity threshold defined as the three upcoming stops in the driver's active route. Entry into this window triggers customer notification." },
      { "term": "Stop Duration", "definition": "The estimated time a driver spends at each delivery stop. Defaults to 4 minutes but is overridable per zone by the operations team." },
      { "term": "Deviation", "definition": "When a driver's actual GPS position or stop sequence diverges from the system-suggested route beyond a defined threshold, triggering recalculation." },
      { "term": "Pending Delivery", "definition": "A delivery that has been created and is awaiting assignment to a driver's route. Only pending deliveries in the driver's zone are candidates for route assignment." },
      { "term": "Failed Delivery", "definition": "A delivery that was attempted but could not be completed (e.g., recipient unavailable). Removes the stop from the active route and triggers recalculation of remaining stops." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A driver must never be assigned a delivery whose package requirements exceed their licensed vehicle class capacity or type.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a physical and potentially legal constraint. A motorcycle physically cannot carry freight. Violating this corrupts the assignment model and may violate transport regulations.",
      "testable_assertion": "For every delivery on a driver's route, delivery.requiredVehicleClass must be compatible with driver.licensedVehicleClass."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically compute and assign an optimized route from all pending deliveries in the driver's assigned zone.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is an operational policy — the business could decide to allow manual route creation or batch assignment at different times. The automation is a business decision, not a data integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Within T seconds of a driver's shift starting, a route containing all eligible pending deliveries in their zone is computed and assigned to the driver."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "The route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's active zone.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is an optimization policy. The business could choose to batch new deliveries or defer them to the next shift. Failing to recalculate does not corrupt state — it just produces a suboptimal route.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a delivery is created with a zone matching an active driver's zone, a RouteRecalculationRequested event is emitted and the driver's route is updated to include the new delivery."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The route must be recalculated when a delivery on the active route is marked as failed.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational policy for efficiency. The system would not be corrupt without recalculation — just suboptimal.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a delivery's status transitions to 'failed', the failed stop is removed from the route and remaining stops are re-optimized."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "The route must be recalculated when a driver manually deviates from the suggested route.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational policy to maintain route relevance. The definition of 'deviation' itself is a configurable threshold, confirming this is policy.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a DeviationDetected event is raised for a driver, the remaining undelivered stops are re-optimized from the driver's current position."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window of the driver's active route.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Notification policy. The window size (3) is a business preference that could change. Missing a notification does not corrupt delivery state.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a delivery moves into position 1, 2, or 3 of the driver's ordered route, exactly one notification is sent to the customer for that delivery."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "The default estimated stop duration is 4 minutes, overridable per zone by the operations team.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "A configurable parameter. Both the default and the override mechanism are pure business policy.",
      "testable_assertion": "Route ETA calculations use the zone-specific stop duration if configured, otherwise 4 minutes. The operations team can update the zone override without a code change."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "A driver can only be assigned deliveries within their assigned zone for the current shift.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Zone assignment is a partitioning invariant. Cross-zone assignment would break route optimization assumptions, create dispatch conflicts between drivers, and could violate operational SLAs tied to zones.",
      "testable_assertion": "For every delivery on a driver's route, delivery.zone must equal driver.currentShift.zone."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "A delivery that has already been completed or is assigned to another active driver's route must not be assigned to a second driver's route.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Double-assignment corrupts the delivery lifecycle. Two drivers arriving at the same address for the same package is a data integrity failure.",
      "testable_assertion": "At any point in time, a delivery appears on at most one active route, and completed deliveries appear on zero active routes."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-10",
      "rule": "Recalculation must preserve already-completed stops and only re-optimize remaining undelivered stops.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Completed deliveries are historical facts. Recalculation that reorders or removes completed stops corrupts delivery history and could re-trigger customer notifications.",
      "testable_assertion": "After any route recalculation, all previously completed stops retain their original position and status; only stops with status 'pending' or 'in-transit' are subject to reordering."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A driver starts their shift and there are 5 pending deliveries in their zone, all compatible with their vehicle class",
      "when": "The shift is activated",
      "then": "An optimized route containing all 5 deliveries is assigned to the driver within the defined SLA window"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A driver starts their shift and there are 5 pending deliveries in their zone, but 2 require a freight vehicle and the driver has a motorcycle",
      "when": "The shift is activated",
      "then": "An optimized route containing only the 3 compatible deliveries is assigned; the 2 freight deliveries remain unassigned"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A driver is mid-route with 4 remaining stops",
      "when": "A new pending delivery is added to their zone that is compatible with their vehicle class",
      "then": "The route is recalculated to optimally include the new delivery among the remaining stops; completed stops are untouched"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A driver is mid-route and delivery at stop #3 is marked as failed",
      "when": "The failure is recorded",
      "then": "Stop #3 is removed from the active route and remaining stops are re-optimized from the driver's current position"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A driver skips their next suggested stop and proceeds to a different location",
      "when": "The system detects the deviation",
      "then": "The route is recalculated from the driver's actual current position with all remaining undelivered stops"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A driver has 5 remaining stops and completes stop #1",
      "when": "The customer at stop #4 was previously outside the next-3-stops window and is now at position #3",
      "then": "Exactly one notification is sent to the customer at new position #3 informing them their delivery is approaching"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A customer has already received a 'next 3 stops' notification",
      "when": "A route recalculation moves them out of and then back into the next-3-stops window",
      "then": "The customer is NOT sent a duplicate notification"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "The operations team has configured a zone with a 6-minute stop duration override",
      "when": "A route is calculated for a driver in that zone with 10 stops",
      "then": "The route ETA reflects 60 minutes of stop time (10 × 6 min) plus travel time, not 40 minutes (10 × 4 min)"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "Two drivers are active in the same zone",
      "when": "Routes are assigned at shift start",
      "then": "No delivery appears on both drivers' routes simultaneously"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A driver starts their shift in Zone A",
      "when": "There are pending deliveries in Zone A and Zone B",
      "then": "Only Zone A deliveries are considered for the driver's route"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Driver starts shift with zero pending deliveries in their zone", "expected_behavior": "An empty route is created. As new deliveries arrive in the zone, recalculation trigger (BR-3) creates a route. The driver is not left in a broken state." },
    { "scenario": "All pending deliveries in a zone require a vehicle class no active driver possesses", "expected_behavior": "Deliveries remain unassigned. An alert should be raised to the operations team indicating unserviceable deliveries due to vehicle class mismatch." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple recalculation triggers fire simultaneously (new delivery added + deviation detected)", "expected_behavior": "Recalculations must be serialized or idempotent — the system must not produce duplicate routes or race conditions. The final route state must reflect all trigger inputs." },
    { "scenario": "A delivery is added to a zone after the driver has already passed the optimal insertion point geographically", "expected_behavior": "The optimizer includes it at the best remaining position. It must never be silently dropped." },
    { "scenario": "Driver deviates but then returns to the original route before recalculation completes", "expected_behavior": "Recalculation should still complete using the driver's position at the time of computation. The system must not cancel in-flight recalculations based on stale position assumptions." },
    { "scenario": "Customer notification is triggered but customer has no valid contact method on file", "expected_behavior": "Notification fails gracefully. The delivery proceeds normally. Notification failure must not block route progression." },
    { "scenario": "Operations team sets stop duration override to 0 minutes for a zone", "expected_behavior": "System should reject or floor the value. A 0-minute stop duration is physically impossible and would produce misleading ETAs." },
    { "scenario": "A route recalculation occurs while the driver is currently at a stop (delivering)", "expected_behavior": "The current stop is treated as in-progress and excluded from reordering. Recalculation optimizes only stops after the current one." },
    { "scenario": "Two drivers start shifts in the same zone at nearly the same time", "expected_behavior": "Delivery-to-driver assignment must be atomic. No delivery is double-assigned. The system must handle concurrent route generation with proper concurrency control." },
    { "scenario": "A driver's shift ends while deliveries remain on their route", "expected_behavior": "Undelivered stops revert to 'pending' status and become eligible for reassignment to another driver or the next shift. Customer notifications for those stops should indicate a delay, not imminent arrival." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Optimized routing directly reduces fuel costs, increases deliveries-per-shift (driver utilization), and reduces failed delivery costs by dynamically adapting to failures. Incorrect vehicle class assignment could result in regulatory fines and wasted trips. ETA accuracy affects customer retention and repeat purchase rates.",
    "operational": "This feature centralizes route decisions, reducing dispatcher workload. However, it introduces a critical dependency on the route optimization engine — if it fails or is slow, all shift starts are blocked. Recalculation frequency directly impacts system load; high-volume zones could cause computational spikes. The 4-minute default and zone overrides require a configuration management workflow for the ops team.",
    "user": "Drivers get clear, optimized routes reducing cognitive load and decision fatigue. Customers get proactive notifications improving delivery experience and reducing 'where is my package' support calls. However, frequent recalculations mid-route could frustrate drivers if the route changes too often.",
    "risk": "Concurrency risk: simultaneous recalculation triggers and multi-driver zone assignment are the highest-risk technical scenarios. Domain risk: the spec does not define what 'deviation' means quantitatively — this is a critical gap. Notification risk: duplicate or premature notifications erode customer trust. Regulatory risk: vehicle class enforcement failure could have legal consequences depending on jurisdiction.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "Primary users. Shift start becomes automated. Route changes mid-shift could be disorienting if too frequent. Vehicle class enforcement protects them from being assigned physically impossible deliveries." },
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Receive proactive delivery notifications. ETA accuracy depends on correct stop duration configuration. Notification spam (duplicates) or false proximity alerts would degrade trust." },
      { "group": "Operations Team", "impact": "Gains zone-level stop duration configuration power. Needs tooling to manage overrides. Must monitor for unserviceable deliveries (vehicle class gaps). Reduced manual dispatch work." },
      { "group": "Dispatchers", "impact": "Role significantly reduced by automation. May need to handle exceptions (unassignable deliveries, optimizer failures) rather than routine assignment." },
      { "group": "Finance/Management", "impact": "Expects measurable improvements in deliveries-per-shift, fuel cost reduction, and customer satisfaction scores. Needs reporting on route efficiency metrics." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 55,
    "reasoning": "The spec covers the happy path well but has multiple undefined behaviors and ambiguous terms. 'Deviation' is not quantitatively defined. Concurrent multi-driver zone assignment is not addressed. Shift-end behavior for incomplete routes is unspecified. Notification idempotency is not mentioned. The relationship between vehicle classes and delivery requirements lacks a classification scheme. These gaps affect implementability and correctness.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Vehicle class enforcement is a hard invariant — clearly stated and unambiguous",
      "Recalculation triggers are well-enumerated (3 specific events)",
      "Stop duration default and override mechanism is clear",
      "Next-3-stops notification window is precisely defined"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Definition of 'deviation' — what distance/time threshold? What if GPS is inaccurate?",
      "Multi-driver concurrency in the same zone — how are deliveries partitioned?",
      "Behavior when route optimizer fails or times out",
      "Shift lifecycle boundaries — what happens to in-flight routes at shift end?",
      "Notification idempotency — spec does not address duplicate prevention on recalculation",
      "Vehicle class hierarchy — is it a strict hierarchy or a tag system? Can a van driver handle motorcycle-eligible deliveries?"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "A driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift",
      "Vehicle class compatibility is a well-defined mapping (not specified in the requirement)",
      "Route optimization is synchronous enough to not block driver operations for extended periods",
      "The 'next 3 stops' window is evaluated after each route change and each stop completion",
      "Customer notification is at-most-once per delivery per window entry — no duplicate on re-entry",
      "Zone-level stop duration override applies uniformly to all stops in the zone, not per-delivery"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The spec says the route must recalculate when a driver 'manually deviates from the suggested route' but does not define deviation quantitatively. Is it GPS distance from the next stop? Skipping a stop in sequence? Taking an alternate road? Without a precise definition, the deviation detection system cannot be correctly implemented.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Drivers", "Operations Team"],
      "options": [
        "Define deviation as the driver completing a stop out of suggested sequence",
        "Define deviation as GPS position exceeding N meters from the suggested path for M seconds",
        "Define deviation as the driver explicitly requesting a different route"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Use stop-sequence deviation (driver completes a stop out of order or skips one) as the primary trigger, as it is deterministic and does not depend on GPS accuracy. GPS-based path deviation can be added as a secondary trigger with configurable thresholds."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "When multiple drivers are active in the same zone, how are pending deliveries partitioned among them? The spec says 'all pending deliveries in their zone' which implies each driver gets ALL deliveries — clearly wrong. Is it nearest-first? Load-balanced? Capacity-based?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Drivers", "Dispatchers", "Operations Team"],
      "options": [
        "Deliveries are partitioned by geographic proximity to minimize total zone travel",
        "Deliveries are load-balanced evenly across active drivers by count",
        "Deliveries are assigned based on vehicle capacity and delivery size/weight"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Partition by optimized geographic clustering constrained by vehicle capacity. This is the standard logistics approach and aligns with the optimization intent of the feature."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "The vehicle class compatibility model is not defined. Is it hierarchical (a truck can handle van deliveries) or strict (each class only handles its own)? What are the valid vehicle classes? Without this, BR-1 cannot be fully implemented.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Drivers", "Operations Team"],
      "options": [
        "Define a strict hierarchy: freight > truck > van > motorcycle (higher can handle lower)",
        "Define a tag-based system where each delivery specifies required vehicle capabilities",
        "Define strict class matching with no cross-compatibility"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Use a capability-based model where vehicle classes have a set of capabilities (e.g., refrigeration, max weight, max dimensions) and deliveries declare required capabilities. This is the most flexible and domain-accurate approach in logistics."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "Shift-end behavior is unspecified. What happens to deliveries still on a driver's route when their shift ends? Are they automatically reassigned? Does the driver get overtime? Is the customer notified of the delay?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Drivers", "Customers", "Operations Team"],
      "options": [
        "Undelivered stops revert to pending and are reassigned to the next available driver",
        "Driver is prompted to complete remaining stops with overtime approval",
        "Operations team is alerted to manually reassign"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Automatically revert undelivered stops to pending status with an operations alert. Customers who received a 'next 3 stops' notification for now-unassigned deliveries must receive an updated notification."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
